svg.text: Arena-oriented allocation model aligned with design/ast.md and oxc-style ownership

g.text:
"/** Find a user.
 * See {@link UserService} for details.
 * @param {string|number} id - The user ID
 * ... */"
Caller-owned source is separate from arena; all Text / Identifier / Variation string data are zero-copy slices.

svg.text: Sequential alloc() calls; every AST node stores Span and is dropped together when arena is freed.

g.text: BlockTag.raw_body: Box<Text> preserves raw tail for recovery/validation
No dedicated node for @since or @deprecated: semantics stay in GenericTagBody
A lexically special tag such as @borrows would allocate
bump_ptr → next free region; one arena free tears down the entire AST
Conceptual field ordering only. Exact byte size depends on allocator/runtime details.
span: Span | description: Option<Box<Description>> | tags: Vec<BlockTag>
Root owns top-level description pointer and the block-tag vector header.
`raw_body` is retained even when a parsed body exists.
Most tags reduce to type? + value? + description?.
Shared substrate for link targets, type names, and borrows endpoints.
Dedicated param/property path syntax is not reused as general namepath.
TypeExpr variants include Name, Union, TypeApplication, Function, Record,
TypeQuery, IndexedAccess, Nullable, Optional, Variadic, ArrayShorthand ...
Expanded compared with the old diagram: TypeQuery and IndexedAccess now matter.
span | callee: TypeExpr | type_arguments: Vec<TypeExpr> | has_dot: bool
No longer hard-coded to TypeName; callee can be any TypeExpr.
Supports forms like Parameters<Foo>[0] and T["x"].
RecordField { span, key: RecordFieldKey, value: Option<Box<TypeExpr>> }
The AST is designed in an oxc-compatible direction:
arena allocation, #[repr(C)], and Span on every node.
However, raw transfer is still a future phase.
Vec backing layout is not yet treated as a stable JS ABI.
This diagram therefore shows ownership and pointer shape,
not a frozen cross-language binary contract.
Design goal: later raw-transfer adaptation without transport-only fields in the core AST.
Used by link targets, type names, @borrows, and some tag values.
Separated from general namepath to preserve semantic role.
→ Box children and Vec children depending on the variant
Union, TypeApplication, Function, Record, TypeQuery, and IndexedAccess allocate differently.
